questions.prompt_html:
Find the derivative of the function at the given point and interpret the physical meaning of this quantity. Include units in your answer.

The cost C, in dollars, of a rental car driven for m miles is given by C(m) = 25 + 0.16 m. Find C'(50).
questions.prompt_text: Find the derivative of the function at the given point and interpret the physical meaning of this quantity. Include units in your answer. The cost C, in dollars, of a rental car driven for m miles is given by C(m) = 25 + 0.16 m. Find C'(50).

choices.label: A
choices.html: C'(50) = 8 dollars; the instantaneous cost is $8.00 when the car is driven 50 miles.
choices.text: C'(50) = 8 dollars; the instantaneous cost is $8.00 when the car is driven 50 miles.

choices.label: B
choices.html: C'(50) = 0.16 dollars per mile; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $0.16 per mile when the car is driven 50 miles.
choices.text: C'(50) = 0.16 dollars per mile; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $0.16 per mile when the car is driven 50 miles.

choices.label: C
choices.html: C'(50) = 0.16 dollars; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $0.16 when the car is driven 50 miles.
choices.text: C'(50) = 0.16 dollars; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $0.16 when the car is driven 50 miles.

choices.label: D
choices.html: C'(50) = 8 dollars pr mile; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $8.00 per mile when the car is driven 50 miles.
choices.text: C'(50) = 8 dollars pr mile; the cost increases at an instantaneous rate of $8.00 per mile when the car is driven 50 miles.

questions.prompt_html:
Find the derivative of the function at the given point and interpret the physical meaning of this quantity. Include units in your answer.

Suppose that the speed of a train approaching a station is given by v (t) = (t - 8) 2, for 0 ≤ t ≤ 8, where t is measured in seconds and v(t) is measured in meters per second. Find v'(5).
questions.prompt_text: Find the derivative of the function at the given point and interpret the physical meaning of this quantity. Include units in your answer. Suppose that the speed of a train approaching a station is given by v (t) = (t - 8) 2 , for 0 ≤ t ≤ 8, where t is measured in seconds and v(t) is measured in meters per second. Find v'(5).

choices.label: A
choices.html: v'(5) = -3 m/s; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -3 m/s at t = 5 s.
choices.text: v'(5) = -3 m/s; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -3 m/s at t = 5 s.

choices.label: B
choices.html: v'(5) = 6 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is 6 m/s<sup>2</sup> at t = 5 s.
choices.text: v'(5) = 6 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is 6 m/s 2 at t = 5 s.

choices.label: C
choices.html: v'(5) = -3 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -3 m/s<sup>2</sup> at t = 5 s.
choices.text: v'(5) = -3 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -3 m/s 2 at t = 5 s.

choices.label: D
choices.html: v'(5) = -6 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -6 m/s<sup>2</sup> at t = 5 s.
choices.text: v'(5) = -6 m/s per second; the instantaneous rate of change in the train's speed is -6 m/s 2 at t = 5 s.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The amount of a medication (in mg) in the bloodstream t hours after the medication is taken is given by M(t). Estimate the values of M'(1) and M'(6), rounding to the nearest whole number. Include units on your answers and interpret the physical meaning of these values.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The amount of a medication (in mg) in the bloodstream t hours after the medication is taken is given by M(t). Graph f(x) = cos-1 x together with its first derivative. Comment on the behavior of f and the shape of its graph in relation to the signs and values of f'.
questions.prompt_text: Provide an appropriate response. Graph f(x) = cos -1 x together with its first derivative. Comment on the behavior of f and the shape of its graph in relation to the signs and values of f'.
questions.solution_html: The graph of f(x) = cos<sup>-1</sup> x has negative slope on its domain [-1, 1]. Therefore all values of the first derivative are below the x-axis. The graph of f(x) becomes gradually less steep as the graph of f' approaches its vertex at x = 0. To the right of the y-axis, the graph of f' quickly descends and f(x) becomes more steep.

Electrical systems are governed by Ohm's law, which states that V = IR, where V = voltage, I = current, and R = resistance. If the current in an electrical system is decreasing at a rate of 5 A/s while the voltage remains constant at 22 V, at what rate is the resistance increasing (in Ω/sec) when the current is 40 A? (Do not round your answer.)

Solve the problem. Round your answer, if appropriate.

An inverted conical water tank with a height of 12 ft and a radius of 3 ft is drained through a hole in the vertex. If the water level drops at a rate of 1 ft/min, at what rate is water (in ft3/min) draining from the tank when the water depth is 8 ft? (Hint: Use similar triangles.)
